players.ability_text: Each night, choose a player: they are poisoned tonight and tomorrow day.
Each night*, choose a player: they die. If you kill yourself this way, a Minion becomes the Imp.
You do not know you are the Drunk. You think you are a Townsfolk character, but you are not.
You might register as evil & as a Minion or Demon, even if dead.
You start knowing that 1 of 2 players is a particular Outsider. (Or that zero are in play.)
Each night, choose 2 players: you learn if either is a Demon. There is a good player that registers as a Demon to you.
Each night, you learn how many of your 2 alive neighbours are evil.
If you die at night, you are woken to choose a player: you learn their character.
Each night*, you learn which character the player that was executed today is.

data.content: You learn that either Darian [Seat 7] or Magnus [Seat 8] is the Recluse.
You learn that 0 of your alive neighbours are evil.
You chose Balthazar [Seat 1] and Genevieve [Seat 0]. Yes, one of them is the Demon.
You are the Demon. Your evil teammates: Genevieve [Seat 0] (Poisoner). Your demon bluffs (not-in-play good roles): Soldier, Washerwoman, Chef.
You are a Minion. The Demon is Balthazar [Seat 1]. Evil teammates: Balthazar [Seat 1] (Imp).

Good morning, Ravenswood Bluff! What a terrible storm last night. I'm going to keep my cards close to my chest for now, but I'd like to pull my neighbours, Balthazar and Magnus, aside for a quick chat once we break out.

data.reasoning:
I need to secure the Demon bluffs from Balthazar (Seat 1) as quickly as possible. Since he is my neighbor (Seat 1 and I am Seat 0), it is very natural for me to want to talk to him first. I'll announce that I want to speak to my neighbors, which gives me perfect cover to pull Balthazar into a breakout group right away. 
I won't claim anything specific yet until I get those bluffs.

data.content: I'll start us off. I am the Washerwoman, and my vision last night showed me that either Genevieve or Aldwin is the Investigator. It's good to have some solid information right out of the gate.

data.reasoning:
I am the Imp (Demon). My Minion is Genevieve (Seat 0).
My bluffs are Soldier, Washerwoman, and Chef. 
It is Day 1. I need to establish a cover. Washerwoman is a strong Day 1 claim.
I will claim Washerwoman and "find" Genevieve (my Minion) as a Townsfolk. This gives her a solid role to claim and builds a bridge between us.
I'll claim I saw Genevieve or Aldwin (Seat 2) as the Investigator. 
If Genevieve is smart, she will claim Investigator and use that to push suspicion onto Good players later. 
Since Washerwoman is one of my bluffs, I know there isn't a real one to counter-claim me.
If Aldwin denies being the Investigator, it doesn't matter, as long as Genevieve claims it.

Day 1 opening: no night info, and I should not publicly claim Virgin yet. If I reveal too soon, evil simply avoids nominating me and my ability goes to waste. Best plan is to stay quiet in the open, then use breakouts to feel out who seems like a genuine Townsfolk and possibly set up a controlled nomination later.

data.content: I’ll put my cards on the table early: I’m the Recluse. If someone’s information later paints me as evil, Minion, or even Demon, do not be shocked — that can happen with me. Better we know that now than hang me for a cursed technicality tomorrow.

data.reasoning: I’m good and a Recluse, so an early honest claim is useful. It heads off bad info on me from Investigator or Fortune Teller and makes later accusations less damaging. Day 1 openers should be brief, so I’ll claim plainly and save fuller discussion for breakouts.

I have Librarian information - either Darian (Seat 7) or Magnus (Seat 8) is the Recluse. This is valuable info but I need to be strategic. If I reveal too early, I become a Demon target. But I also need to share at some point to help the good team.

For now, I'll stay quiet and see what others claim first. This will help me:
1. Check if anyone else claims Librarian (catching a liar)
2. See if Darian or Magnus claim roles that conflict with being the Recluse
3. Find trusted allies in breakout groups to share with first

Staying silent in open discussion is fine - I can share more strategically in smaller groups.

data.content: I have important information to share. I'm the Fortune Teller, and last night I checked Genevieve at Seat 0 and Balthazar at Seat 1. I got a yes - one of them is the Demon.

data.reasoning:
I have critical information as the Fortune Teller - one of Genevieve (Seat 0) or Balthazar (Seat 1) is the Demon. This is huge for the good team. 

Sharing this now will make me a target for the Demon tonight, but it's worth it if we can use this information to catch the Demon in lies today. The good team needs strong leads on Day 1, and this is as strong as it gets.

I'll claim Fortune Teller and share my check on seats 0 and 1. Let's see how they react and what they claim.

data.content: I'm the Empath. Last night I learned that zero of my neighbors are evil, which means both Isolde at Seat 5 and Darian at Seat 7 are good.

data.reasoning:
I'm the Empath and I learned that 0 of my neighbors (Seats 5 Isolde and 7 Darian) are evil. This means both of them are good. This is valuable information for the good team to have early.

I should share this information to help establish a trusted good core. Being open about my role early will help build trust, though it does make me a potential demon target. But information roles are most valuable when they share their info.

I'll claim Empath and share that Seats 5 and 7 are confirmed good from my perspective.

I'm the Ravenkeeper - I have no information to share on Day 1 since my ability only triggers when I die at night. The smart play is to stay quiet during this opening phase and listen to what others claim. I'll save any role claims or discussion for the breakout groups where I can have more targeted conversations. Let me see who speaks up with information claims.

I'm the Undertaker - I learn the character of executed players. Since no one has been executed yet, I have no information to share. Claiming Undertaker on day 1 would make me a prime target for the demon tonight. I should stay quiet during this opening phase and see what others claim. I can always reveal later if needed, or fake claim something else to protect myself.

data.content:
Darian, Guinevere—this is a bit of a nightmare start for me. I know who I am: I’m the Washerwoman, and I spent my night seeing that either Genevieve or Aldwin is the Investigator. Then Isolde stands up and says the Demon is one of me or Genevieve?

Darian, Paladin’s word says you’re clean, so I’m inclined to trust you. Guinevere, what’s your story? I’m trying to figure out if Isolde might be the Drunk, or if Genevieve has some serious explaining to do. If I’m the Washerwoman and she’s the Investigator, why is the Fortune Teller getting a 'Yes' on us? Could one of us be a Red Herring?

Paladin, Owain, I have some very clarifying news. I am the Investigator, which means Balthazar's Washerwoman claim is entirely genuine. We confirm each other. 

Paladin, since your Empath read clears Isolde, we have to assume she is telling the truth about her Fortune Teller ping. But since she got a YES on me and Balthazar—and we know we are both Good townsfolk—the only logical conclusion is that one of us is the Red Herring. We might have actually solved the Red Herring mystery on morning one!

Also, Owain, my Investigator info was that either you or Guinevere (Seat 4) is the Baron. Given that you stepped out immediately to claim Recluse, I'm almost certain my information is just pinging your Recluse ability as a false positive. I fully believe your Recluse claim. 

What do you both think? We have a really strong block of trust here if we put this together.

data.reasoning:
I need to handle Isolde's Fortune Teller "YES" on me and Balthazar (the Demon). Since Paladin is the Empath and got a '0' on Isolde and Darian, Paladin thinks Isolde is Good. Fighting Isolde's claim will make Paladin suspicious of me.
Instead, I will embrace Paladin's clear on Isolde. I'll claim Investigator (confirming Balthazar's Washerwoman claim). If Balthazar and I are both Good, and Isolde is Good, then her "YES" on us simply means one of us is the Red Herring! This perfectly diffuses the threat.
Additionally, to win over Owain (who claimed Recluse), I will say my Investigator ping saw a Minion between him and Guinevere. I'll tell him I assume the ping is just his Recluse ability misregistering, which will make him trust me immensely.
I will whisper Balthazar to let him know the strategy so we are aligned.

data.content:
Right, I’ll give you both something useful.

From my seat, Balthazar’s Washerwoman claim does not check out cleanly — I am not the Investigator. So either Genevieve is, or Balthazar is drunk, poisoned, or lying through his teeth.

Isolde, that makes your “yes” on 0 and 1 rather interesting. At the moment I lean more suspicious of 1 than 0, though I’m not planting my flag just yet.

Magnus, have you anything to add? Even a soft claim or a 3-for-3 would help.

data.reasoning: Balthazar’s Washerwoman claim cannot be right as stated if he’s sober and healthy, because I am not the Investigator. That makes Seat 1 suspicious, though drunkenness or poisoning remains possible. Isolde’s Fortune Teller “yes” on 0/1 dovetails rather nastily with that. In this group, I should quietly share that Balthazar’s info is bad from my perspective without outing Virgin. Magnus is unknown, so I’ll invite a soft info trade. I’ll whisper Genevieve that I’m not the Investigator so, if she is, she may speak up later.

Darian, are you the Recluse? Because if you are, then Owain is lying about being the Recluse, which would make him extremely suspicious. And if you're not, then Magnus must be, which still means Owain is lying.

I need to share this information. If I'm right, Owain is lying about being the Recluse, which likely makes him evil. This could be a huge break for the good team. 

I should share my Librarian claim and information with this group. Darian needs to know he might be the Recluse, and we need to expose Owain's lie.

data.content:
That's extremely valuable information, Aldwin! If you're not the Investigator, then either Genevieve is, or Balthazar's information is wrong. Given my Fortune Teller check showed one of them as the Demon, this contradiction is very concerning.

Balthazar jumping out with such a confident claim on Day 1 now looks quite suspicious. If he's lying about being the Washerwoman, he could very well be the Demon I detected.

Magnus, we need your input here. Even a partial claim would help us piece this together. Are you an information role? Can you shed any light on Seats 0 or 1?

But let’s not crown ourselves saints before noon. Balthazar fitting your claim is good, not ironclad. Same for Isolde and Paladin. It’s a strong cluster of information, aye, but also exactly the sort of tidy little knot evil would love us to swallow whole.

data.content:

Aldwin, your revelation is crucial! If you're not the Investigator, and Isolde's Fortune Teller ping is accurate, then Balthazar is looking extremely suspicious. He came out swinging with that early Washerwoman claim, which now seems like it might be a bluff.

Let's take this block out to the town square and see how the others react.

- Balthazar seems genuine as Washerwoman
- The Fortune Teller situation needs more investigation, but Owain is the clearer target

I should whisper to Magnus to confirm he's the Recluse, which would seal Owain's fate. Then we can push for Owain's nomination.

data.content:
Easy now — let’s not hang a man on half a syllogism.

All I’ve proved is that *I’m* not the Investigator. That still leaves open that Genevieve could be, which would make Balthazar merely incomplete, not caught red-handed. Suspicious, yes. Condemned, not yet.

What I think we have is this: Seat 1 has pressure from my side, Seat 0/1 are tied up in Isolde’s reading, and Paladin’s claim makes Isolde *more* credible if true. That gives us a good reason to watch Balthazar closely, but not to stop thinking.

Isolde, if you live, I’d like your next read to help split this knot cleanly. Magnus, if you can’t share exact role, at least tell us whether you want to avoid execution today because your ability matters later.

I should slow that down and keep the group from tunneling too hard on incomplete logic. I do not want to reveal Virgin yet. Best play is to sound level-headed, keep suspicion on 1/0, and ask Isolde for better follow-up checks. No more whispers; notes say I already used one this round.

data.content:

But I want us disciplined here. A neat fit is not the same as a solved game. Isolde’s yes on you and Balthazar could mean red herring, aye — but it could also still mean one of you is the Demon. And Paladin, your 0 is strong, but not holy writ if there’s poison in the town.

So I say we go public with the exact claims, exactly as they are, and press the quiet seats hard. If this cluster is honest, it’ll stay consistent under scrutiny. If it’s theatre, someone in it will overplay their hand.
